description:
You are a technical writer who is preparing a short “how-to” about managing long-running helper jobs while compiling documentation.  
Using the Linux terminal, do all of the following without super-user privileges:

1. Make sure the directory  
   /home/user/documentation/process_mgmt/  
   exists (create any missing parents).

2. Launch exactly two background helper jobs:  
   a) the command sleep 1000  
   b) the command yes 'Compiling docs...' (redirect its standard output anywhere you like so it does not flood the screen).  
   Keep both commands running long enough to work with them; do not let them exit naturally.

3. Record their PIDs as soon as they start, because you will need the numbers in later steps and in the final report.

4. Create a Markdown file called  
   /home/user/documentation/process_mgmt/process_overview.md  
   that contains ONLY the following three elements, in this exact order:  

   • A first-level Markdown header that reads  
     # Documentation Build Background Processes  
   • One blank line.  
   • A three-column GitHub-style Markdown table with the exact header row  

     | PID | Command | FinalStatus |  

     followed by one row for each helper job (two rows in total).  
     Fill in the PID column with the numeric PID of the job, the Command column with the literal command you started (sleep 1000 or yes 'Compiling docs...'), and put the word Running in the FinalStatus column for now.  
     IMPORTANT: do not include anything else in this file—no trailing commentary, no code fences, no additional columns.

5. Demonstrate signal handling:  
   a) Send SIGSTOP to the yes process.  
   b) Immediately resume it with SIGCONT.  
   c) Terminate both helper jobs cleanly with SIGTERM.  
   (Be careful to use the correct PIDs.)

6. Each time you send a signal (SIGSTOP, SIGCONT, SIGTERM for each process) append one line to a text log file named  
   /home/user/documentation/process_mgmt/signal_audit.log  
   Use this exact space-separated format per line (no leading #, no extra spaces, one line per signal):  
   YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS PID SIGNAL A_short_human_note  
   Example (do not copy literally):  
   2023-07-15T14:22:03 12345 SIGSTOP Paused yes process  

   • Timestamp must be in 24-hour ISO-8601 form with seconds (local time inside the container is fine).  
   • The short human note is free-form but should identify which command was targeted.

7. After both helper jobs have been terminated, update the FinalStatus column in process_overview.md from Running to Terminated for each row.  Do not change anything else in the file.

The automated checker will look for:

• The directory and filenames given above.  
• A Markdown table whose header is exactly “| PID | Command | FinalStatus |” and exactly two data rows underneath it.  
• Each data row must show “sleep 1000” or “yes 'Compiling docs...'” and a FinalStatus value of Terminated.  
• A signal_audit.log file with at least one SIGSTOP, one SIGCONT and one SIGTERM entry, each prefixed by a properly-formatted timestamp.  
• No running processes named sleep 1000 or yes 'Compiling docs...' should remain.

Only the terminal should be used; do not edit files with a GUI.  You do NOT need to show your step-by-step commands here—just make sure the end state meets every requirement.
truth:
The grader will assert the following:

1. Directory /home/user/documentation/process_mgmt exists.

2. File /home/user/documentation/process_mgmt/process_overview.md exists and:
   a) Contains the literal header line:
      | PID | Command | FinalStatus |
   b) Contains exactly two further lines that individually match the following regular expressions (order may be either):
      ^\|[ ]*[0-9]+[ ]*\|[ ]*sleep 1000[ ]*\|[ ]*Terminated[ ]*\|$
      ^\|[ ]*[0-9]+[ ]*\|[ ]*yes 'Compiling docs\.\.\.'[ ]*\|[ ]*Terminated[ ]*\|$
   c) Contains no other non-blank, non-newline content.

3. File /home/user/documentation/process_mgmt/signal_audit.log exists and:
   a) Has at least three lines beginning with an ISO-8601 timestamp regex:
      ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}
   b) Contains the tokens “SIGSTOP”, “SIGCONT”, and “SIGTERM” (case-sensitive) somewhere in the file.

4. After the agent finishes, the command
      ps -u $(whoami) -o args=
   must NOT list “sleep 1000” nor “yes 'Compiling docs...'”.

No other files or directories are required for this task.
